PHASE 1 · COMMUNITY LAUNCH

Early entry holders maximize Phase 2 profits here.

Why being first matters more than being right.

$42DAY launches on Pump.fun — a Solana launchpad where every buyer is equal and price discovers itself as the community builds. There's no presale. No insider rounds. No allocations to influencers.

The earlier you arrive, the cheaper your bag. The bigger your bag, the more you'll compound when Phase 2 Auto Earn opens after Raydium graduation.

1B total supply $69K graduation target 100% liquid
01

The lowest entry price $42DAY will ever have

Pump.fun launches start at the bottom and discover price as buyers arrive. Every buyer pays more than the one before. Phase 1 holders are buying at the floor — by definition.

02

Your Phase 1 bag is your Phase 2 ticket

When $42DAY graduates to Raydium, the Auto Earn vault opens. Every token you hold qualifies for deposit. Bigger Phase 1 bag, bigger Phase 2 position. There's no other way to buy in cheaper than now.

03

Supply is locked. Forever.

Mint authority renounced before launch. 1,000,000,000 $42DAY exist. No new tokens can ever be created — by anyone, including us. The math doesn't move.

04

Phase 1 stacks the deck for Phase 3

Top Phase 1 holders by bag size qualify for the Ticka NFT collection and the Year 3 loyalty airdrop. Phase 3 is built specifically to reward the people who showed up early.

What is Pump.fun?

Pump.fun is a Solana launchpad where tokens start with zero allocation, no presale, and no insider rounds. Every buyer is equal. Price discovers itself as the community builds. If $42DAY hits the graduation threshold (~$69K market cap), it auto-migrates to Raydium with full liquidity — and Phase 2 begins.

NEXT · PHASE 2 · AUTO EARN
PHASE 2 · AUTO EARN

Let Ticka get to work for you.
He works, you earn.

After Raydium graduation, the vault opens. Your bag goes to work.

Deposit. Earn. Compound. Claim.

The Auto Earn vault activates when $42DAY graduates to Raydium. Holders deposit voluntarily — there's no lockup forced on you, no time limit to enter, no minimum.

Once you deposit, every 28-day cycle pays you rewards from a pool of 175 million tokens. The longer you wait, the more cycles accumulate. The more you compound, the bigger your share gets.

"Set it. Forget it. Watch it grow."

Four mechanics. One compounding machine.

Each one solves a problem most yield products ignore.

01

Auto-Compound: rewards earn rewards

When you claim, 30% lands liquid in your wallet — tradeable instantly. The other 70% creates a new vesting bucket inside the vault. That bucket earns its own rewards next cycle. Nobody else on Solana does this.

30% LIQUID
70% COMPOUNDS
02

Backlog Claim: your share waits

Skip a cycle. Skip a year. Your share is locked in the math from the day you deposit. Claim everything accumulated whenever you're ready. No "use it or lose it." No pressure to time the market.

CLAIM ANY TIME · NEVER MISS A CYCLE
03

Finite Allocation: we tell you the end date

175M $42DAY is the entire rewards pool. Up to 7% per cycle for the first two years, then a linear taper through year three. Most projects hide the end date. We put it in the marketing.

175M POOL · ~26 CYCLES FLAT · 13 CYCLES TAPER
04

Vesting: patience pays

Each compounded bucket vests on a schedule. 30% unlocks at 3 months. 60% at 6 months. 100% at 12 months. Withdraw any unlocked amount whenever you want, after the milestone hits.

3 MONTHS
30%
6 MONTHS
60%
12 MONTHS
100%

The Reward Schedule

175M $42DAY rewards pool distributes across three years. Up to 7% per cycle for the first two years (~26 cycles of 28 days each), then a linear taper through year three. Year four and beyond: market dynamics.

Anti-gaming protection

Tier position is averaged across multiple snapshots approaching year three — not a single point-in-time check. A last-minute whale deposit won't jump the queue. Real holders are protected.

Stack early. Deposit smart. Get rewarded.

Tier position is decided by your total vault balance — the deposit you put in plus everything that compounded on top of it. The bigger you stack in Phase 1, the bigger you compound in Phase 2, the higher you rank in Phase 3.

NEXT · QUESTIONS & ANSWERS
QUESTIONS & ANSWERS

The questions a smart buyer actually asks.

No softballs. If we can't answer honestly, we don't answer.

01
What if I miss Phase 1 and buy after Raydium graduation?

You can still deposit into the Auto Earn vault — Phase 2 is open to anyone who holds $42DAY, whenever they buy.

The trade-off is price. Phase 1 buyers got the lowest entry $42DAY will ever have. Post-graduation buyers pay the market price set by Phase 1 demand. You can still compound, you can still rank for Phase 3 — you just paid more per token than the people who showed up first.

02
What does "up to 7%" actually mean for me?

7% per cycle is the maximum the contract pays. Actual rate depends on total vault TVL — the 175M rewards pool is fixed, so more depositors means each individual depositor's share scales accordingly.

If TVL stays small, depositors get closer to 7%. If TVL grows large, the rate per depositor scales down so the pool lasts the full term. The total reward distribution across all depositors is what's fixed, not your individual rate.

Plain: it's not a guaranteed return. It's a fair share of a known pool.

03
What happens if the rewards pool runs out faster than 3 years?

It can't. The rate is designed around a fixed 175M pool over a fixed cycle count — the contract enforces the schedule. If TVL is high, the rate per depositor scales down to make the pool last. If TVL is low, depositors get closer to the maximum rate but the pool still lasts the full term.

The contract makes the math impossible to break. The end date is real and pre-coded.

04
Can the team rug? What stops them?

The team holds 1.5% — 15M of 1B. That's a deliberately small allocation. We can sell our tokens on the open market like anyone else, but we can't pull liquidity, can't mint new tokens, can't change the contract.

Mint authority is renounced before launch. The contract is non-upgradeable. The LP is the LP. Whatever happens, the structure stays the structure.

The honest answer: no project can prove a future, but we can show you the constraints. Read Section 7 for the receipts.

05
What if I deposit but want my tokens back?

Your initial deposit isn't locked — you can withdraw it from the vault at any time.

Compounded rewards (the 70% that auto-buckets when you claim) are on the vesting schedule: 30% unlocks at 3 months, 60% at 6 months, 100% at 12 months. Once a milestone hits, you withdraw any unlocked amount whenever you want.

No early exit penalty. No forced lockup on principal.

06
What happens after Phase 3 — does $42DAY just die?

The Phase 2 rewards distribution ends at year three. The Phase 3 airdrop lands. After that, $42DAY operates on market dynamics — just like any other token at maturity.

What stays alive: the LP, the holders, the Ticka NFTs in the top 10 wallets, the broader community and marketing programs.

What doesn't continue: the structured reward program. That's the honest answer. We told you the end date because we're not selling perpetual yield — we're selling a defined 3-year program plus what comes after.

07
Is the contract audited?

The contract has been tested end-to-end on Solana devnet — 10 mechanics verified live, including the full reward cycle, the taper transition, multi-cycle backlog claims, and auto-compound bucketing.

A formal third-party audit is on the roadmap post-launch, funded from the marketing allocation. We'd rather tell you what's true than oversell what's planned.

See Section 7 for the full devnet verification list and contract addresses.

08
Why should I trust this over the other 24,000 daily meme coin launches?

You don't have to. The math is on-chain and renounced. The team holds 1.5%. The reward pool has a disclosed end date. The Phase 3 tiers are pre-coded.

99% of meme coins go to zero because they have no plan beyond launch. $42DAY has three phases, finite tokenomics, and a contract that does what we say it does. Either that's enough or it isn't.

We're not asking you to believe us. We're asking you to read the contract.

Still have a question?

Ask us on Telegram or X. We answer publicly — no DMs, no private deals.
